label: 124_sanctions_ruble_russian_russias
description: The financial and trade sanctions imposed on Russia following its invasion of Ukraine have significantly affected the Russian economy. These sanctions, among the most powerful in recent history, target key sectors including the ruble and banking system, aiming to pressure political and business leaders into changing policies. The effectiveness of such measures remains a subject of ongoing debate.

earliest_articles.title: Russia’s tit for tat
earliest_articles.excerpt: Since 16 March 2014, the EU (in concerted action with the US) has frozen assets and imposed travel bans on 33 persons and an individual bank. On 20 March, Russia counteracted with the reciprocal blacklisting of EU and US officials. This pattern of tit for tat raises the question of the comparative vulnerability of the…

earliest_articles.title: Are Russia's Usable Reserves Running Dangerously Low?
earliest_articles.excerpt: Russia is heading toward a serious financial squeeze next year, despite the confident statements of President Vladimir Putin. The official figures accounting for Russia's international reserves show a dramatic decline in international reserves, but an examination of the numbers reveals that the situation is far worse…

earliest_articles.title: Barry Bosworth on the Effect of Sanctions on Russia’s Economic Slide
earliest_articles.excerpt: Russian officials are saying that falling energy prices will push their nation’s economy into recession in 2015. Russian President Vladimir Putin, who earlier this week canceled a new natural gas pipeline to Europe, is expected to unveil his economic agenda later this week. newest_articles.title: Why Russia's economic model no longer delivers
newest_articles.excerpt: At the St. Petersburg Economic Forum in early June—once a prestigious Russian annual gathering for global investors, now a more insular event[1]—a panel featuring Russia’s finance minister, economy minister and central bank governor addressed the country’s economic outlook. newest_articles.title: Russia’s missing payments
newest_articles.excerpt: Since the invasion of Ukraine, Western countries have imposed an unprecedented wave of economic sanctions on Russia. This includes freezing Russia’s official reserves, ejecting several Russian banks from international payments, imposing a cap on the price of its oil exports, and imposing restrictions on critical and…

central_articles.title: Russian sanctions: Some questions and answers
central_articles.excerpt: Russia’s invasion of Ukraine is altering global security and economic relationships (Snower 2022). In this column, we focus on the financial and trade sanctions imposed on Russia. The sanctions on Russia are the most powerful and costly punishments imposed on a major economy at least since the Cold War. Their speed,…

central_articles.title: Can Sanctions Stop Putin?
central_articles.excerpt: Editor’s Note: Clifford Gaddy and Barry Ickes article “Can Sanctions Stop Putin?” is also available to read in Russian. central_articles.excerpt: There is a large literature on the rationale and impacts of the imposition of economic sanctions.
